creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
I had spotted another neat in the new code: sometimes it can create
broken /etc/resolv.conf when IP address of the nameserver is missing.
In my case it was due to the unupdated /etc/defaults/rc.conf, but some
other curcumstances can give rise to this bug.

The following patch checks that IP is non-empty string.
--- resolv.7.patch begins here ---
From 12777d468989dce44e751af0e3d58f11dc9f1d33 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Eygene Ryabinkin <rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru>
Date: Sat, 25 Oct 2008 23:19:28 +0400
Subject: [PATCH] Do not add 'nameserver' lines with empty server IP

I had not updated my /etc/defaults/rc.conf and had found that the
resulting /etc/resolv.conf will be broken.  So I am skipping empty
nameserver IPs.

Signed-off-by: Eygene Ryabinkin <rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru>
---
 etc/rc.d/resolv |    5 +++--
 1 files changed, 3 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/etc/rc.d/resolv b/etc/rc.d/resolv
index 18782a2..648e62b 100644
--- a/etc/rc.d/resolv
+++ b/etc/rc.d/resolv
@@ -54,13 +54,14 @@ build_resolv () {
 	fi
 
 	if checkyesno resolv_build_named_forwarders &&
-	    checkyesno named_enable; then
+	    checkyesno named_enable &&
+	    [ -n "$resolv_named_ip" ]; then
 		echo "nameserver $resolv_named_ip"
 	fi
 
 	set -- "$2"
 	for ns in `IFS=', '; echo $*`; do
-		echo nameserver $ns
+		[ -n "$ns" ] && echo nameserver "$ns"
 	done
 
 	if [ -f /etc/resolv.conf.tail ]; then
-- 
1.6.0.2
--- resolv.7.patch ends here ---
-- 
Eygene

 `-'" `"\_  ,_.-;_.-\_ ',  fsc/as   #
     _.-'_./   {_.'   ; /           #    -- FreeBSD Developers handbook 
    {_.-``-'         {_/            #
